ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general pizza food product class (FOODON_00002426); no specific Napoletana variant exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers pizza as a category; no specific Napoletana vegan variant code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226695008 covers pizza generically; no Napoletana-specific concept exists. Closest parent used.

main_ingredients: pizza dough (wheat flour, water, yeast, salt)

serving.natural_language_portion: One whole Pizza Napoletana as typically served in a restaurant, weighing approximately 300 g, consisting of a thin Neapolitan-style crust topped with tomato sauce, capers, black olives, and garlic.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pizza, cheese, frozen, baked (SR Legacy 21299) adapted for vegan Napoletana; supplemented with USDA component data for capers (NDB 02054), black olives (NDB 09193), and tomato sauce (NDB 11549)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for vegan Pizza Napoletana. Values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for thin-crust pizza dough base with component adjustments for capers, black olives, garlic, and tomato sauce. Cheese is absent (vegan). Energy and macros are consistent with published thin-crust vegan pizza literature. Values represent a reasonable per-100 g estimate for this preparation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; thin-crust pizza analogue (GI ~53, glucose reference)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for vegan Pizza Napoletana. GI of 53 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for thin-crust pizza (GI ~53, glucose reference), which is the closest analogous food. The absence of cheese (lower fat) may slightly raise GI compared to cheese pizza; however, the olive oil and fibre from olives and capers moderately offset this. GI 53 is retained as the best defensible estimate. GL per serving = (53 × 35.5 × 300) / (100 × 100) = 56.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; ~1560 mg per full serving) from capers, olives, and tomato sauce
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one or two slices and request reduced-salt preparation, as a full serving provides approximately 1560 mg sodium, representing over 65% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load (35.5 g per 100 g) with low GI of 53 due to thin crust and olive oil content
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low glycemic index (GI 53) and moderate dietary fibre (2.4 g per 100 g) support a moderate postprandial glucose response; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit intake to one to two slices and pair with a non-starchy vegetable side to manage total glycemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium from capers and olives; however, monounsaturated fat from olive oil is cardioprotective
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The monounsaturated fat profile from olive oil (approximately 3.2 g per 100 g) is consistent with a Mediterranean dietary pattern and is cardioprotective, but the high sodium content from capers and olives warrants portion control for individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pizza Napoletana (Vegetarian, Vegan) is a traditional Neapolitan-style pizza originating from Naples, Italy, prepared without animal-derived toppings. This vegan variant features a thin, hand-stretched wheat dough base baked at high temperature, topped with a rich tomato sauce, brined capers, black olives, and garlic, finished with a drizzle of olive oil. The crust is characteristically soft and slightly charred at the edges, with a chewy interior. The flavour profile is bold and savoury, with the brininess of capers and olives complementing the acidity of the tomato sauce and the pungency of garlic. As a vegan preparation, it contains no cheese or animal products, making it suitable for vegan and dairy-free diets. Nutritionally, it provides approximately 218 kcal per 100 g, with carbohydrates as the dominant macronutrient (35.5 g per 100 g) and a low glycemic index of approximately 53, making it a moderate-GI meal option. The sodium content is notably high (~520 mg per 100 g) due to capers and olives, which is a key consideration for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The olive oil contributes heart-healthy monounsaturated fats. This dish is a staple of Italian cuisine and is widely available in Italian restaurants in Singapore, including at Ricciotti - The Riverwalk.
common_user_queries: What is on a Napoletana pizza?
How many calories in a Napoletana pizza?
What is the GI of Napoletana pizza?
Is Napoletana pizza suitable for diabetics?
What is the sodium content of Napoletana pizza?
What is the difference between Napoletana and Margherita pizza?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for vegan Pizza Napoletana exists in any approved primary source (HPB, USDA SR Legacy, ICMR-NIN). Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for thin-crust pizza dough, tomato sauce, capers, black olives, and garlic, with cheese removed. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (thin-crust pizza, GI 53) in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and component data; no exact approved-source entry exists for vegan Pizza Napoletana. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a plant-based preparation with no hydrogenated fats; confirmed zero per USDA component data. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a fully vegan preparation containing no animal products. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a fully vegan preparation; no animal-derived ingredients are present. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero in this preparation; no fortified ingredients or animal products are present. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and are approximate. 7. GI of 53 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (thin-crust pizza); no direct measurement exists for this specific vegan variant. 8. Glycemic load per serving = (53 × 35.5 × 300) / (100 × 100) = 56.5 g, based on a 300 g whole-pizza serving. 9. This dish is Italian cuisine; multilingual naming rules for Singaporean dishes do not apply. No mandatory local-language names are required for Italian cuisine under the Veritas v2 multilingual matrix. 10. Sodium is high due to capers and black olives; clinical guidance for hypertension and cardiovascular disease reflects this. 11. The food is served at Ricciotti - The Riverwalk, Singapore, sourced from field survey data.
